title: Carbon tax acceptance in a polarized society: Bridging the partisan divide over climate policy in the US
authors: Aitor Marcos, José M. Barrutia, Patrick Hartmann

tax_mentions.context: Study 1 and Study 2 assess fairness perceptions and acceptance of a carbon tax among Democrats and Republicans.

tax_mentions.exact_term_used_in_paper: carbon tax is a fair policy because it raises money that can be returned to taxpayers
tax_mentions.context: Distributional fairness framing presents the carbon tax with lump-sum payments and redistribution.

inclusion_reason: Direct survey experiments analyze carbon tax fairness perceptions and acceptability, including partisan differences and framing effects on support for a carbon tax with revenue recycling.
